about.title: About Re-Bali
about.description: Re-Bali is Bali's trusted classifieds platform for buying and selling second-hand goods. Whether you're an expat relocating, a local entrepreneur, or a business upgrading equipment — Re-Bali connects you with the right people.
about.missionText: To create a sustainable, trusted, and inclusive marketplace for the Bali community — reducing waste, making quality goods accessible, and connecting people across cultures.
about.whatWeDoText: Re-Bali is a free online classifieds platform serving the island of Bali and surrounding areas. We enable individuals and businesses to list items for sale or rent, discover goods and services, and communicate safely through our built-in messaging system. We do not participate in or facilitate payments between users.
about.whoWeServeText: Our community includes expats, digital nomads, local Balinese residents, and businesses of all sizes. Whether you're furnishing a new villa, selling a scooter before leaving the island, or growing your business — Re-Bali is here for you.
about.trustText: Verified sellers, trust scores, and community moderation keep transactions safe. We encourage meeting in public places and inspecting items before purchase.
about.sustainabilityText: Giving items a second life reduces waste and supports a circular economy in Bali. Every resale is one less item in a landfill.
about.communityText: Built by and for the diverse Bali community — locals, expats, and businesses collaborating across cultures.
about.inclusivityText: Available in 12 languages with support for all currencies. Everyone is welcome regardless of nationality, language, or background.
about.transparencyText: No hidden fees, no commissions. Our platform is 100% free to use for basic listings. We are clear about what data we collect and why.
about.fairTradeText: We enforce honest pricing, accurate descriptions, and penalize deceptive practices. Bait pricing and misleading listings are strictly prohibited.
about.legalEntityText: Re-Bali is operated as a digital platform serving the Bali community. For legal inquiries, please contact us through our Contact page. Re-Bali complies with Indonesian law, including the Personal Data Protection Law (UU PDP No. 27/2022).

safety.title: Safety Tips
safety.intro: Your safety is our priority. Follow these guidelines to protect yourself when buying or selling on Re-Bali.
safety.meetPublicDesc: Always meet buyers or sellers in well-lit, public areas such as coffee shops, co-working spaces, or shopping centers. Avoid isolated locations and never invite strangers to your home for a first meeting.
safety.inspectFirstDesc: Always check the item in person before completing any payment. Test electronics, check for damage, and verify that the item matches the listing description and photos.
safety.securePaymentDesc: Prefer bank transfers (BCA, Mandiri, BNI) or trusted e-wallets (GoPay, OVO, Dana). Never send money in advance to someone you haven't met. Be wary of requests for cryptocurrency or wire transfers.
safety.tooGoodToBeTrueDesc: If a deal seems too good to be true, it probably is. Extremely low prices, pressure to act quickly, or requests to move communication off-platform are red flags. Report suspicious listings immediately.
safety.verifyIdentityDesc: Check the seller's profile for verification badges, account age, completed deals, and reviews. Sellers with higher trust scores and verified phone numbers are more reliable.
safety.keepCommsOnPlatformDesc: Use Re-Bali's built-in messaging system for all initial communication. This protects your privacy and allows us to help resolve disputes if needed.
safety.reportSuspiciousDesc: If you encounter a scam, fake listing, or abusive behavior, use the Report button on the listing or user profile. Our moderation team reviews all reports within 24 hours.
safety.protectPersonalInfoDesc: Never share your password, bank account details, or personal documents with other users. Re-Bali staff will never ask for your password or financial information.
safety.emergencyText: In case of fraud or criminal activity, contact Indonesian police (110) or the nearest police station. For consumer protection issues, contact BPKN (National Consumer Protection Agency). You can also report to us via the Contact page.

rules.title: Community Rules
rules.intro: These rules ensure Re-Bali remains a safe, fair, and trusted marketplace for everyone. Violations may result in listing removal, account suspension, or permanent ban.
rules.rule1Desc: Describe items accurately and honestly. Include the real condition, any defects, and all relevant details. Misleading descriptions are grounds for removal.
rules.rule2Desc: Do not list illegal, counterfeit, stolen, or prohibited items. This includes drugs, weapons, endangered species products, and items violating Indonesian law.
rules.rule3Desc: Do not post duplicate listings or spam the platform. Each item should have a single listing. Repeated violations will result in account suspension.
rules.rule4Desc: Treat all users with respect. Harassment, hate speech, discrimination, threats, or abusive language are strictly prohibited and will result in an immediate ban.
rules.rule5Desc: Free accounts are limited to 5 active listings (3 for accounts less than 7 days old). Professional accounts have higher limits based on their plan.
rules.rule6Desc: All listings must include at least one real photo of the actual item. Stock photos, screenshots from other platforms, and photos with added text overlays are not allowed.
rules.rule7Desc: Prices must be accurate and reflect the actual asking price. Bait pricing (advertising a low price to attract buyers then demanding more) is prohibited.
rules.rule8Desc: Listing descriptions must not contain external URLs, phone numbers, WhatsApp numbers, or references to other platforms. Use the built-in contact system.
rules.rule9Desc: List items in the correct category and subcategory. Intentionally miscategorizing items to gain visibility is not allowed.
rules.rule10Desc: Any attempt to defraud other users — including fake listings, advance-fee scams, phishing, or impersonation — will result in an immediate permanent ban and may be reported to law enforcement.
rules.rule11Desc: Each person may have only one account. Creating multiple accounts to circumvent bans, listing limits, or gain unfair advantages is prohibited.
rules.rule12Desc: All users must comply with Indonesian law, including tax obligations (NPWP for professional sellers), consumer protection regulations, and the Personal Data Protection Law (UU PDP).
rules.consequencesText: Violations are handled progressively: first offense results in a warning and listing removal, second offense in a temporary suspension (7-30 days), and third offense in a permanent ban. Severe violations (fraud, illegal items, threats) result in an immediate permanent ban. Re-Bali cooperates with Indonesian law enforcement when criminal activity is detected.

trustPage.title: Trust & Badges
trustPage.subtitle: Learn how we calculate trust scores and award badges to build a safer community.
trustPage.scoreExplanation: Every user receives a trust score from 0 to 100, calculated automatically based on their activity, deals, and verifications. A higher score means the user is more trustworthy.
trustPage.badgesExplanation: Badges are awarded automatically based on your activity, deals, and trust score. They appear on your profile and seller page to help buyers trust you.
trustPage.pointsExplanation: Points are a reward currency earned by unlocking badges and completing actions. Spend them in the Points Shop to boost your listings and profile.
trustPage.pointsEarnExplanation: Synchronize your badges in the Points Shop to earn points. Each badge unlocked grants you a one-time point reward.
trustPage.dynamicEarningDesc: You also earn points automatically when you complete deals, receive 5-star reviews, or submit validated reports against scammers.
trustPage.monthlyCap: Monthly cap: 200 pts from activity (resets each month)

legal.termsIntro: These Terms of Use govern your access to and use of the Re-Bali platform. By creating an account or using our services, you agree to be bound by these terms. Please read them carefully.
legal.privacyIntro: This Privacy Policy explains how Re-Bali collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal data in compliance with Indonesian law, including the Personal Data Protection Law (UU PDP No. 27/2022).

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.text: By accessing or using Re-Bali, you accept and agree to be bound by these Terms of Use and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you must not use the platform. These terms apply to all users — buyers, sellers, and visitors — regardless of location or account type.

description.title: Service Description
description.text: Re-Bali is a free online classifieds platform that connects buyers and sellers in Bali, Indonesia. We provide tools for listing items, discovering goods and services, and communicating between users. Re-Bali does not participate in, facilitate, or guarantee transactions between users. We are not a party to any sale, purchase, rental, or exchange conducted through the platform.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
eligibility.text: You must be at least 18 years old to use Re-Bali. By creating an account, you represent that you are of legal age and have the legal capacity to enter into binding agreements under Indonesian law. Minors may not create accounts or use the platform without parental supervision.

account.title: User Accounts
account.text: You must provide accurate, current, and complete information when creating an account. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your credentials and for all activities conducted under your account. Each person may have only one account. Phone verification via WhatsApp is required to post listings. Re-Bali reserves the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms, without prior notice.

listings.title: Listing Rules
listings.text: Listings must be accurate, honest, and comply with Indonesian law (including Law No. 8/1999 on Consumer Protection). Prohibited items include but are not limited to: illegal drugs, weapons, counterfeit goods, stolen property, endangered species products, pornographic material, and any items prohibited under Indonesian regulations. Photos must represent the actual item. Maximum listing limits apply based on account type and age.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Conduct
prohibited.text: Users must not: post misleading or fraudulent content; harass, threaten, or discriminate against other users; attempt to circumvent platform security measures; use the platform for money laundering or terrorist financing; post external links, phone numbers, or contact information in listing descriptions; create multiple accounts; engage in price manipulation or bait-and-switch tactics; or violate any applicable local, national, or international law.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual.text: All content on Re-Bali (logos, design, code, text) is protected by intellectual property laws. Users retain ownership of content they upload but grant Re-Bali a non-exclusive, worldwide license to display, reproduce, and distribute such content on the platform. Users must not upload content that infringes third-party intellectual property rights.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.text: Re-Bali is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, express or implied. To the maximum extent permitted by Indonesian law, Re-Bali shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from the use of the platform, including but not limited to: loss of profits, data, or goodwill; damages resulting from transactions between users; or unauthorized access to user accounts. All transactions are conducted at the user's own risk.

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.text: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Re-Bali, its operators, employees, and partners from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including legal fees) arising from your use of the platform, your violation of these terms, or your violation of any third-party rights.

governing.title: Governing Law & Jurisdiction
governing.text: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Indonesia. Any disputes arising from these terms or your use of Re-Bali shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in Denpasar, Bali, Indonesia. Before filing any legal action, the parties agree to attempt resolution through good-faith negotiation for a period of 30 days.

modification.title: Modifications
modification.text: Re-Bali reserves the right to modify these terms at any time. Material changes will be announced via email or in-app notification at least 14 days before taking effect. Continued use of the platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated terms. If you disagree with any changes, you must stop using the platform and delete your account.

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: For questions about these Terms of Use, please contact us through our Contact page or email us. Response times are typically within 48 business hours.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.text:
We collect the following categories of personal data:

• Account data: email address, display name, profile photo, account type, preferred language.
• Verification data: phone number (for WhatsApp verification), government-issued ID and selfie (for identity verification, encrypted and auto-purged after review).
• Listing data: photos, descriptions, prices, location, category.
• Communication data: messages exchanged through the platform.
• Device data: IP address, browser type, operating system, device fingerprint (for security).
• Usage data: pages visited, search queries, interaction timestamps.

usage.title: How We Use Your Data
usage.text:
Your data is processed for the following purposes:

• Providing and maintaining the platform and its features.
• Verifying your identity and building trust (trust scores, badges).
• Displaying your listings and enabling communication between users.
• Detecting and preventing fraud, scams, and platform abuse.
• Sending important notifications (deal updates, security alerts).
• Improving our services through anonymized analytics.
• Complying with legal obligations under Indonesian law.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.text:
Under the Indonesian Personal Data Protection Law (UU PDP No. 27/2022), we process your data based on:

• Consent: provided when you create an account and accept these terms.
• Contractual necessity: to provide the services you requested.
• Legitimate interest: fraud prevention, platform security, and service improvement.
• Legal obligation: compliance with Indonesian law, tax regulations, and law enforcement requests.

storage.title: Data Storage & Retention
storage.text: Your data is stored securely on Supabase infrastructure with encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Data centers are located in Southeast Asia. Account data is retained as long as your account is active. ID verification documents are automatically purged within 30 days of review. After account deletion, personal data is removed within 30 days, except where retention is required by law.

sharing.title: Data Sharing
sharing.text:
We share your data only in the following circumstances:

• Public profile: display name, avatar, and listing details are visible to all platform users.
• WhatsApp number: shared only when you explicitly choose to reveal it to another user.
• Service providers: Supabase (hosting), Xendit (payments) — under strict data processing agreements.
• Law enforcement: when required by Indonesian law, court order, or to protect user safety.

We never sell your personal data to third parties for marketing purposes.

cookies.title: Cookies & Tracking
cookies.text:
We use essential cookies only:

• Authentication cookies to maintain your login session.
• Preference cookies to store your language and theme settings.

We do not use third-party advertising cookies or tracking pixels. Analytics data is collected anonymously and cannot be used to identify individual users.

rights.title: Your Rights (UU PDP)
rights.text:
Under the Indonesian Personal Data Protection Law, you have the right to:

• Access: request a copy of your personal data.
• Correction: update or correct inaccurate data.
• Deletion: request deletion of your account and associated data.
• Portability: export your data in a machine-readable format.
• Withdrawal: withdraw consent for data processing at any time.
• Objection: object to processing based on legitimate interest.
• Complaint: lodge a complaint with the relevant Indonesian authority.

To exercise these rights, visit your Profile page or contact us through the Contact page.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.text: Re-Bali is not intended for users under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors. If we discover that a minor has created an account, we will immediately delete the account and all associated data.

security.title: Security Measures
security.text:
We implement comprehensive security measures including:

• AES-256 encryption at rest for all sensitive data.
• TLS 1.3 encryption for all data in transit.
• Device fingerprinting and VPN detection for fraud prevention.
• Automated content moderation and image hashing.
• Rate limiting and brute-force protection.
• Two-factor authentication (TOTP or email).
• Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments.

Despite these measures, no system is 100% secure. We encourage users to use strong passwords and enable 2FA.

international.title: International Data Transfers
international.text: Your data may be processed in countries outside Indonesia for hosting and infrastructure purposes. In such cases, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place as required by UU PDP, including contractual data processing agreements with all third-party providers.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.text: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Material changes will be notified via email or in-app notification at least 14 days before taking effect. The 'Last updated' date at the top reflects the most recent revision.

contact.title: Contact & Data Protection
contact.text: For privacy-related questions, data access requests, or to exercise your rights under UU PDP, please contact us through our Contact page. We aim to respond to all data protection inquiries within 14 business days.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Re-Bali marketplace

q1.a: Re-Bali is a trusted, free marketplace for buying and selling goods and services in Bali, Indonesia. We connect expats, locals, and businesses through secure in-app messaging.

q2.q: How do I post a listing?
q2.a: Sign up, verify your WhatsApp, click 'Publish', fill in details, upload photos, and your listing goes live instantly. Free accounts can post up to 5 listings.

q3.a: Buyers send messages through our built-in chat. Your phone number stays private until you explicitly share it.

q4.a: Agree on a price, meet in person, exchange the item and payment. The seller then closes the deal in-app, the buyer confirms, and both parties can leave reviews.

q5.a: Yes! Posting listings and messaging is 100% free. Optional premium features (boosts, Pro plans) can be purchased with points or IDR.

q1.q: How does the trust score work?
q1.a: Your trust score (0-100) is calculated from account age, completed deals, positive reviews, and verification status. Penalties apply for reports, VPN abuse, and multi-accounting.

q2.a: We use content moderation, duplicate image detection, phone/ID verification, device fingerprinting, VPN detection, and automated flagging. Listings with 3+ scam reports are auto-archived.

q3.a: Yes. Visit their profile and tap 'Block'. Their listings and messages will be hidden. You can unblock from Settings > Blocked Users.

q4.q: How do I report a suspicious listing?
q4.a: Tap the 'Report' button on any listing. Choose a reason and provide details. Our team reviews all reports within 24 hours.

q1.a: Re-Bali does not process payments between users. You arrange payment directly (cash, bank transfer, e-wallet). We recommend meeting in person before paying.

q2.a: Points are a reward currency earned through badges, completed deals, and reviews. Spend them on boosts, seller status upgrades, and extra listing slots.

q3.q: Can I buy points with real money?
q3.a: Yes! Point packs are available via Xendit (bank transfer, e-wallet, QRIS). Prices are in IDR.

q1.q: How do I verify my WhatsApp?
q1.a: Go to Profile > Security, enter your WhatsApp number, and verify with the 6-digit code sent to you. This earns the WhatsApp Verified badge and 20 points.

q2.a: Upload a government-issued ID (KTP, passport) and a selfie. Our team reviews it within 48 hours. Documents are encrypted and auto-deleted after review.

q3.a: Yes. We comply with Indonesia's Personal Data Protection Law (UU PDP). Documents are encrypted (AES-256), auto-purged after review, and never shared. See our Privacy Policy and PDPL page.

q1.q: How do I change my display name or avatar?
q1.a: Go to Profile > Personal Information and update your name or upload a new photo.

q2.q: What's the difference between Private and Business accounts?
q2.a: Private accounts are for individuals (up to 5 listings). Business (Pro) accounts get more listings, analytics, a business page, and optional NPWP verification.

q3.q: How do I delete my account?
q3.a: Go to Profile > Security > Delete Account. All your data will be permanently removed within 30 days, as required by Indonesian PDP law.

q1.a: Re-Bali operates under Indonesian law, including UU ITE (Electronic Information and Transactions), UU PDP (Personal Data Protection), and Consumer Protection Law No. 8/1999.

q2.a: UU PDP (Law No. 27/2022) is Indonesia's Personal Data Protection Law, similar to GDPR. It gives you rights over your data including access, correction, deletion, and portability. See our PDPL page for details.

q3.q: How do I exercise my data rights?
q3.a: Visit Profile > Security or our Contact page. You can request data access, correction, export, or deletion. We respond within 14 business days.

pdpl.title: PDPL Compliance (UU PDP)
pdpl.intro: This page explains how Re-Bali complies with Indonesia's Personal Data Protection Law (Undang-Undang Perlindungan Data Pribadi — UU PDP No. 27/2022), which is the Indonesian equivalent of the European GDPR.
pdpl.lawReference: Reference: Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection (UU PDP), enacted October 17, 2022, with full enforcement effective October 17, 2024.

overview.title: Overview
overview.text: Re-Bali is committed to protecting the personal data of all users in accordance with UU PDP No. 27/2022. This page supplements our Privacy Policy with specific information about our compliance with Indonesian data protection law.

scope.title: Scope of Application
scope.text: UU PDP applies to all processing of personal data conducted by or for Re-Bali, whether the data subjects are located in Indonesia or abroad. This includes data processed through our website, mobile application, and any third-party services we use.

dataController.title: Data Controller
dataController.text: Re-Bali acts as the Data Controller (Pengendali Data Pribadi) for all personal data processed through the platform. As Data Controller, we determine the purposes and means of data processing and are responsible for ensuring compliance with UU PDP.

dataCollected.title: Personal Data Collected
dataCollected.text:
We collect and process the following categories of personal data as defined by UU PDP:

• General Personal Data: name, email address, phone number, profile photo, listing content, device information, usage data.
• Specific Personal Data: government-issued identification documents (KTP, passport) submitted for identity verification — classified as sensitive data under UU PDP and subject to enhanced protection measures.

processingBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
processingBasis.text:
Under Article 20 of UU PDP, we process your personal data based on:

• Explicit Consent (Art. 20(a)): obtained when you create an account, accept our Terms of Use, and submit verification documents.
• Contractual Performance (Art. 20(b)): necessary to provide the services you requested.
• Legal Obligation (Art. 20(c)): compliance with Indonesian tax law, anti-money laundering regulations, and law enforcement requests.
• Legitimate Interest (Art. 20(d)): fraud prevention, platform security, and service improvement, balanced against your rights and freedoms.

dataSubjectRights.title: Your Rights Under UU PDP
dataSubjectRights.text:
As a data subject (Subjek Data Pribadi), you have the following rights under UU PDP:

• Right to Information (Art. 5-6): know what data we collect, why, and how it is processed.
• Right to Access (Art. 7): request a copy of your personal data.
• Right to Correction (Art. 8): update or correct inaccurate data.
• Right to Deletion (Art. 9): request deletion of your data when it is no longer necessary.
• Right to Withdraw Consent (Art. 10): withdraw your consent at any time.
• Right to Object (Art. 11): object to processing based on legitimate interest.
• Right to Portability (Art. 12): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
• Right to Compensation (Art. 12): claim compensation for damages caused by data protection violations.

To exercise these rights, visit Profile > Security or contact us via the Contact page. We will respond within 3×24 hours and fulfill requests within 14 business days.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.text:
In accordance with UU PDP Article 25, we retain personal data only as long as necessary:

• Account data: retained while your account is active. Deleted within 30 days of account deletion.
• Verification documents: encrypted at rest, auto-purged within 30 days of review completion.
• Transaction data: retained for 5 years as required by Indonesian tax law.
• Security logs: retained for 1 year for fraud prevention purposes.
• Anonymized analytics: retained indefinitely (cannot identify individuals).

crossBorder.title: Cross-Border Data Transfer
crossBorder.text:
Under UU PDP Article 56, cross-border transfer of personal data requires adequate protection. Our infrastructure provider (Supabase) processes data in Southeast Asian data centers. We ensure all cross-border transfers comply with UU PDP requirements through:

• Contractual safeguards (Data Processing Agreements).
• Verification that the receiving country provides adequate data protection.
• Technical measures (encryption) to protect data during transfer.

breach.title: Data Breach Notification
breach.text:
In accordance with UU PDP Article 46, in the event of a personal data breach that may affect your rights and freedoms, we will:

• Notify affected data subjects within 3×24 hours of becoming aware of the breach.
• Notify the relevant supervisory authority within the same timeframe.
• Provide details of the breach, potential impact, and remedial actions taken.
• Take immediate steps to mitigate the impact and prevent recurrence.

dpo.title: Data Protection Officer
dpo.text:
In accordance with UU PDP requirements, Re-Bali has designated a Data Protection Officer (DPO) responsible for:

• Monitoring compliance with UU PDP and internal data protection policies.
• Advising on data protection impact assessments.
• Serving as a point of contact for data subjects and supervisory authorities.
• Handling data protection inquiries and complaints.

contact.title: Contact & Complaints
contact.text:
For data protection inquiries, to exercise your rights, or to file a complaint:

• In-app: Visit Profile > Security or use our Contact page.
• Response time: 3×24 hours for acknowledgment, 14 business days for fulfillment.

If you are not satisfied with our response, you have the right to file a complaint with the relevant Indonesian supervisory authority as established under UU PDP.
